name: NGO & Development Management
student_summary: Run the organizations solving India's hardest problems.
overview: Run the organizations solving India's hardest problems. NGO & Development Management sits in the Development Sector family of Social Impact, Psychology & Community Services. It is a broad career direction — you can enter it through several routes and specialize later without restarting.
who_is_this_for: You naturally step up when a group needs direction
who_should_avoid: You strongly prefer solitary desk work over this field's day-to-day reality
daily_responsibilities: Apply programme management to real problems
Collaborate with your team using fundraising
Keep improving at m&e — the field keeps moving
Communicate progress, results and decisions to the people who depend on them
problem_solving_style: Adaptive — mix analysis, action and people skills as the situation demands
scope: Demand outlook in India is booming; see FAQs for how the platform keeps this current.
advantages: Demand is booming: opportunities across India and beyond
A broad Development Sector foundation that opens multiple specializations later
challenges: Early years demand patience before the rewards compound
misconceptions: “You must pick your exact job title now.” NGO & Development Management is a direction — specialization comes later.
“Only toppers make it.” Consistent skill-building beats one exam rank in the long run.
portfolio_ideas: A documented case study of fundraising
projects: Beginner: explore programme management with a small self-chosen project
Intermediate: build or run something real using MIS
smaller_city_scope: Viable in smaller cities — demand exists well beyond the metros.
rural_scope: Limited rural presence; expect to base yourself in a town or city.
home_state_note: Ask the AI coach about your state — regional demand, cities and relocation advice are generated live from retrieved data, never hardcoded.
transition_paths: Toward Social Work: build the shared skills and take a bridging project or course
Toward CSR & Corporate Social Impact: build the shared skills and take a bridging project or course
Toward Fundraising & Grant Writing: build the shared skills and take a bridging project or course
faqs: What does NGO & Development Management pay in India?
Entry roles typically start around ₹4 LPA and senior professionals reach ₹15 LPA or more. Live, region-specific salary data is retrieved by the platform when you ask.
What should I study after Class 12?
Focus on Social Science, Economics. Typical degrees: Any degree + MA Development/MSW, IRMA/APU/TISS. Alternatives exist: any degree + msw.
Will AI take over this career?
AI impact is minimal — deeply human/physical work. Broad paths like NGO & Development Management evolve rather than disappear — the students who pair domain skill with AI tools benefit most.
Is this a broad path or a specific job?
NGO & Development Management is a career direction. Once inside it you can specialize further — the platform will map specializations in future versions.
